reasoning: The Lean encoding expresses exactly "P ∩ H is the unique Sylow p-subgroup of H." The conjunction combines (a) `∀ R : Sylow p H, R.toSubgroup = (H ⊓ P.toSubgroup).subgroupOf H` (every Sylow p-subgroup of H coincides with P ∩ H viewed inside H), with (b) `Nonempty (Sylow p H)` (existence). Together these imply `(H ⊓ P).subgroupOf H` is itself a Sylow p-subgroup (via some R) and that it is the unique one — matching the informal claim. `(H ⊓ P.toSubgroup).subgroupOf H` unfolds to `H ∩ P` pulled back via the subtype inclusion, i.e. the canonical Mathlib encoding of "P ∩ H as a subgroup of H". The `P.Normal` hypothesis and `Fintype G`/`Fintype H` surface the implicit finiteness premise of the Dummit-Foote context needed for the claim's truth; the `Nonempty` conjunct is redundant (Mathlib has a global instance) but not incorrect. No hypotheses are silently dropped or added beyond what the informal statement requires.
